B as land belonging to a private house
C as a shared area set up by the local community

questions.content: Why is there a statue of Diane Gosforth in the park?
options: A she was a resident who helped to lead a campaign
B she was a council member responsible for giving the public access
C she was a senior worker at the park for many years

questions.content: During the first World War the park was mainly used for
When did the physical transformation of the park begin?

Which TWO groups of people is the display primarily intended for?

B residents of the local area

questions.content: What are Cathy and Graham’s TWO reasons for choosing the novelist Charles Dickens?
options: A his speeches inspired others to try to improve society
B he used his publications to draw attention to social problems
C his novels are well know now
D he was consulted on a number of social issues
E his reputation has changed in recent times

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Agricultural programme in Mozambique
How the programme was organised
• It focused on a dry and arid region in Chicualacuala district near the Limpopo river
• People depended on the forest to provide charcoal as a source of income
• (31) _______ was seen as the main priority to ensure the supply of water
• Most of the work organised by farmer’s associations was done by (32) _______
• Fenced areas were created to keep animals away from crops
• The programme provided
o (33) _______ for the fences
o (34) _______ for the suitable crops
o Water pumps
• The farmers provided
o Labour
o (35) _______ for the fences on their land
Further developments
• The marketing of produce was sometimes difficult due to lack of (36) _______
• Training was therefore provided in methods of food (37) _______
• Farmers made special places where (38) _______ could be kept
• Local people later suggested keeping (39) _______
Evaluation and lessons learned
• Agricultural production increased improving incomes and food security
• Enough time must be allowed, particularly for the (40) _______ phase of the programme

questions.content:
Agricultural programme in Mozambique
How the programme was organised
• It focused on a dry and arid region in Chicualacuala district near the Limpopo river
• People depended on the forest to provide charcoal as a source of income
• (31) {{BLANK}} was seen as the main priority to ensure the supply of water
• Most of the work organised by farmer’s associations was done by (32) {{BLANK}}
• Fenced areas were created to keep animals away from crops
• The programme provided
o (33) {{BLANK}} for the fences
o (34) {{BLANK}} for the suitable crops
o Water pumps
• The farmers provided
o Labour
o (35) {{BLANK}} for the fences on their land
Further developments
• The marketing of produce was sometimes difficult due to lack of (36) {{BLANK}}
• Training was therefore provided in methods of food (37) {{BLANK}}
• Farmers made special places where (38) {{BLANK}} could be kept
• Local people later suggested keeping (39) {{BLANK}}
Evaluation and lessons learned
• Agricultural production increased improving incomes and food security
• Enough time must be allowed, particularly for the (40) {{BLANK}} phase of the programme
